output:
I’m reviewing story `27-5-simplify-run-ts-remove-hand-tracked-state:quality` as a code review task. I’ll inspect the relevant changes, read the affected code paths against the story intent, and then verify whether correctness, security, architecture, and AC coverage hold up.I’ve confirmed the story is in the current working tree, not a separate branch or commit. Next I’m reading the story contract and the touched workflow/run files to compare the implementation against the stated refactor boundaries and acceptance criteria.The change set is broader than the story text: besides simplifying `run.ts`, it also changes `workflow-runner.ts`, `workflow-visualizer.ts`, and error emission in the story machine. I’m checking those extra changes carefully because that’s where most regression risk sits.I’ve isolated the main review surface now: the `run.ts` simplification itself looks mechanically aligned with the story, so I’m focusing on secondary effects from the new synthetic `workflow-viz` path and the expanded snapshot parser. Those are the areas most likely to hide regressions that the story didn’t explicitly budget for.I have enough static context to validate the design. I’m running a targeted subset of the affected tests now to separate genuine implementation issues from purely theoretical concerns before I finalize the verdict.No findings. The implementation is consistent with the story intent: [run.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/commands/run.ts) removes the hand-tracked workflow/header state, eliminates `updateWorkflowState()` from the sequential path, keeps only cumulative `totalCostUsd` and `storiesDone`, and delegates workflow-row rendering to inspect/synthetic `workflow-viz` events. The added support in [workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts) and [workflow-visualizer.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-visualizer.ts) is coherent with the new single-row visualization path, and I did not find a security issue or concrete architecture break.

AC coverage looks acceptable. AC8-10 are directly satisfied in [run.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/commands/run.ts), AC5 is covered by the renderer heartbeat in [ink-renderer.tsx](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/ink-renderer.tsx), and the affected unit coverage is strong across the updated test files. I reran targeted unit tests for the touched areas and the build; both passed. I did not rerun `npm test` or lint in this turn, but the prior recorded check for this story shows both passing.

<verdict>pass</verdict>
<metrics tests-passed="247" tests-failed="0" lint-warnings="0" issues="0" />
